page.primary_keyword: marbella yacht charter family day itinerary
page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter Family Day Itinerary: Snorkeling, Lunch & Fun

data.meta_description: Plan the perfect marbella yacht charter family day itinerary with snorkeling, lunch & fun. Our 12.5m Astondoa 40 starts at €749/2h from Puerto Banús.
data.summary: A detailed family day itinerary for a Marbella yacht charter, featuring snorkeling stops, a beachside lunch, and kid-friendly activities aboard our Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) ideal for families.
Tier A pricing: €749/2h to €2,299/8h, all-inclusive (skipper, drinks, snacks, VAT).
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's luxury marina.
Snorkeling at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, both calm coves with clear water.
Lunch stop at a beachside chiringuito on the Costa del Sol.
Inclusions: licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, cava, water, insurance.
Maximum 9–11 guests, perfect for multi-generational family groups.
Book via WhatsApp for custom timing and special requests.

faq.q: What is the best boat for a family day charter in Marbella?
faq.a: For families, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal. Both are spacious, have shaded areas, and include a licensed skipper. Pricing starts at €749 for 2 hours and goes up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day, with all drinks, snacks, and fuel included.

faq.q: Where do we depart from for the family yacht charter?
faq.a: All our family charters depart from Puerto Banús, the famous marina in Marbella. You’ll meet the skipper at the dock, and the boat is ready with welcome drinks and safety briefing before you head out.

faq.q: Can we go snorkeling during the itinerary?
faq.a: Yes, snorkeling is a highlight. We recommend stops at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, both sheltered coves with calm, clear waters. We provide snorkeling gear onboard, and the skipper will anchor safely for a swim.

faq.q: Is lunch included in the family day charter?
faq.a: Lunch is not included in the base price, but we can arrange a stop at a beachside chiringuito (e.g., in Cabopino or Estepona) where you can order fresh seafood or paella. Alternatively, you can bring your own picnic. Soft drinks, beer, cava, and snacks are provided.

faq.q: What is the maximum number of guests for a family charter?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 holds up to 9 passengers, and the Azimut 39 up to 11. This includes children and adults. For larger families, consider our Mangusta 80 (12 pax, €4,719 minimum 4h) or Canados 86 (12 pax).

faq.q: What should we bring for the kids?
faq.a: Bring swimsuits, towels, sunscreen (SPF 50+), hats, and a light jacket for wind. We provide snorkeling gear, life jackets for all ages, and a shaded cockpit. Toys or floating devices are welcome but must be approved by the skipper.

faq.q: How do we book a family day itinerary?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. We’ll tailor the itinerary to your family’s preferences—snorkeling, lunch stop, or a cruise past the Golden Mile. All Tier A boats require a 50% deposit to secure the date.

faq.q: What time should we start the family day charter?
faq.a: A 10:00 AM departure works best. You’ll have time for a morning cruise, a snorkeling stop, lunch around 1:00 PM, and a relaxed afternoon swim before returning to Puerto Banús by 6:00 PM. The 8-hour charter gives you a full, unhurried day.

data.body_html:
Planning a marbella yacht charter family day itinerary that balances snorkeling, lunch, and fun is simpler than you think. From Puerto Banús, our Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) offer all-inclusive pricing from €749 for 2 hours to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day. Each charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, cava, water, and light snacks—so you can focus on the family, not the logistics.

Why Choose a Family-Friendly Yacht from Puerto Banús?

Marbella’s coastline is tailor-made for families. Calm coves, shallow waters, and a string of beachside chiringuitos make it easy to keep everyone entertained. Departing from Puerto Banús means you’re minutes from the best spots, and our Tier A boats are spacious enough for multi-generational groups without feeling cramped. The Astondoa 40, for example, has a large sunpad and shaded cockpit—ideal for toddlers and grandparents alike.

Sample Family Day Itinerary: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM

Here’s a proven schedule that works for families with children aged 4–14. Adjust timings as needed, but this gives you a relaxed pace.

  • 10:00 AM – Board at Puerto Banús. Welcome drinks and safety briefing with the skipper.
  • 10:30 AM – Cruise east toward Cabopino. Spot the dunes and Marbella’s Golden Mile from the water.
  • 11:30 AM – Anchor at Cala del Faro for snorkeling. Calm, clear water and rocky outcrops teeming with fish.
  • 1:00 PM – Head to a chiringuito in Cabopino or Estepona for lunch (fresh grilled fish, paella, or kids’ menu).
  • 2:30 PM – Post-lunch swim stop at a second cove, Cala Cortés. Shallow entry for little ones.
  • 4:00 PM – Cruise back toward Puerto Banús, passing the luxury villas of the Golden Mile.
  • 5:30 PM – Dock at Puerto Banús. Ice cream on the marina before heading home.

This itinerary uses an 8-hour charter (€2,299). For shorter outings, a 4-hour trip (€1,499) can include snorkeling and a quick lunch stop.

Snorkeling Spots for All Ages

Two coves stand out for family snorkeling. Cala del Faro (near Cabopino) has a sandy bottom and rocky edges where you’ll see bream and occasional octopus. Cala Cortés (west of Marbella) is more sheltered, with minimal current—perfect for children learning to snorkel. We provide masks, snorkels, and fins onboard. The skipper will anchor in 3–5 metres of water so even non-swimmers can enjoy the view from the boat’s ladder.

Lunch Options: Chiringuito or Picnic?

Most families prefer a beachside lunch at a chiringuito. Popular choices include Chiringuito Cabopino (fresh seafood and paella) or El Ancla in Estepona (kid-friendly menu). The skipper can moor the boat close to the shore, and you’ll wade in or take a tender. Alternatively, bring your own picnic—our boats have a small fridge and table. Soft drinks, beer, and cava are included, so you only need to bring food.

What’s Included in Your Family Charter?

Every charter with us includes: a licensed, English-speaking skipper; fuel for the full itinerary; water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava; light snacks (chips, olives, nuts); insurance; safety equipment (life jackets for all sizes); and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). No hidden costs. The skipper handles navigation, anchoring, and safety, leaving you free to relax.

Which Boat Suits Your Family?

BoatLengthMax GuestsPrice (2h)Price (8h)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749€2,299
Azimut 3912.5m11€749€2,299

Both boats have a shaded cockpit, sunpad, and a small cabin for changing. For larger families (up to 12 guests), consider our Mangusta 80 (24m) starting at €4,719 for 4 hours, which includes a jet ski.

Booking Your Family Day Charter

Ready to secure your date? Contact us via WhatsApp for availability. We recommend booking at least 2 weeks in advance during July and August. A 50% deposit holds the boat, with the balance due 48 hours before departure. We’ll send a detailed itinerary confirmation, including the skipper’s contact and meeting point at Puerto Banús.

For more inspiration, browse our full fleet and pricing page for other family-friendly options.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best boat for a family day charter in Marbella?

For families, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal. Both are spacious, have shaded areas, and include a licensed skipper. Pricing starts at €749 for 2 hours and goes up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day, with all drinks, snacks, and fuel included.

Where do we depart from for the family yacht charter?

All our family charters depart from Puerto Banús, the famous marina in Marbella. You’ll meet the skipper at the dock, and the boat is ready with welcome drinks and safety briefing before you head out.

Can we go snorkeling during the itinerary?

Yes, snorkeling is a highlight. We recommend stops at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, both sheltered coves with calm, clear waters. We provide snorkeling gear onboard, and the skipper will anchor safely for a swim.

Is lunch included in the family day charter?

Lunch is not included in the base price, but we can arrange a stop at a beachside chiringuito (e.g., in Cabopino or Estepona) where you can order fresh seafood or paella. Alternatively, you can bring your own picnic. Soft drinks, beer, cava, and snacks are provided.

What is the maximum number of guests for a family charter?

The Astondoa 40 holds up to 9 passengers, and the Azimut 39 up to 11. This includes children and adults. For larger families, consider our Mangusta 80 (12 pax, €4,719 minimum 4h) or Canados 86 (12 pax).

What should we bring for the kids?

Bring swimsuits, towels, sunscreen (SPF 50+), hats, and a light jacket for wind. We provide snorkeling gear, life jackets for all ages, and a shaded cockpit. Toys or floating devices are welcome but must be approved by the skipper.

How do we book a family day itinerary?

Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. We’ll tailor the itinerary to your family’s preferences—snorkeling, lunch stop, or a cruise past the Golden Mile. All Tier A boats require a 50% deposit to secure the date.

What time should we start the family day charter?

A 10:00 AM departure works best. You’ll have time for a morning cruise, a snorkeling stop, lunch around 1:00 PM, and a relaxed afternoon swim before returning to Puerto Banús by 6:00 PM. The 8-hour charter gives you a full, unhurried day.
